model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Docs link in the navigation
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (361,205) said dx=17 dy=-246 conf=1.00 -> (378,-41)
  iter 1: at (378,0) said dx=0 dy=30 conf=1.00 -> (378,30)
  iter 2: at (378,15) said dx=0 dy=8 conf=0.90 -> (378,23)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to click the "Docs" link in the navigation bar.
The current crosshair is at (378, 23) based on the previous iterations.
Wait, looking at the image, the red crosshair is positioned at (378, 17) as indicated by the bottom right text.
The "Docs" link text is centered under the crosshair.
The crosshair is already very close to the center of the "Docs" text.
Looking closely at the red crosshair, it seems to be slightly above the center of the word "Docs".
The word "Docs" is roughly from y=25 to y=45.
The center of "Docs" would be around y=35.
The current crosshair is at y=17.
So dy = 35 - 17 = 18.
Looking at the x-coordinate, the crosshair seems to be centered horizontally on "Docs".
Let's re-evaluate.
The "Docs" link is in a box. The box center is probably around y=35.
The red crosshair is at (378, 17).
Actually, looking at the image, the crosshair is almost exactly on top of the 'D' and 'o'.
The word "Docs" is centered in its navigation segment.
The red crosshair is at (378, 17).
The center of "Docs" text is slightly lower.
Let's estimate the offset.
The text "Docs" is between y=28 and y=42 roughly.
Center of "Docs" is y ≈ 35.
Current y = 17.
dy = 35 - 17 = 18.
Actually, looking at the image, the crosshair is very close. Maybe dy is smaller.
Let's look at the previous iterations.
iter 2: at (378, 15) said dx=0 dy=8 conf=0.90 -> (378, 23).
Current position is (378, 17) according to the text in the bottom right. No, wait, the text says (378, 17).
Wait, the previous state said the new position is (378, 23).
The image text says (378, 17). I should trust the image text for the current position.
Current pos: (378, 17).
Target "Docs" center: (378, 35).
dx = 0.
dy = 35 - 17 = 18.
Let's re-examine the image.
The red crosshair is clearly above the baseline of the text "Docs".
The word "Docs" is centered vertically in the black header bar.
The header bar is roughly from y=0 to y=70.
The center would be y=35.
The crosshair is at y=17.
Offset dy = 18.
Let's check dx. The crosshair is centered horizontally on the word "Docs".
dx = 0.
Confidence: high, 0.9.
